name: MODG (MOD medium supplemented with 30 mM glucose) (PMC13185565)
description: MODG (MOD medium supplemented with 30 mM glucose) — medium reported in PMC13185565 (complex/undefined base — extracts not BiGG-mappable).

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC13185565 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Full recipe recovered from the primary literature: base medium 'MOD medium (chemically defined medium for Bacillus cereus), used as MODG = MOD + 30 mM glucose' (Rosenfeld E, Duport C, Zigha A, Schmitt P. 2005. Characterization of aerobic and anaerobic vegetative growth of the food-borne pathogen Bacillus cereus F4430/73 strain. Can J Microbiol 51(2):149-158. doi:10.1139/w04-132 (PMID:15920631). MOD originally derived from Glatz BA, Goepfert JM. 1977 and reformulated by Duport et al. 2004 (Arch M
provenance.verification_evidence: grown in MOD medium supplemented with 30 mM glucose (MODG) as the carbon source (32). Anaerobic batch cultures were carried out at 37 °C ... Strict anaerobic conditions (pO2 = 0%) were achieved by continuously sparging the medium with pure nitrogen gas. // The MOD medium used in this study is not supplemented with metal ions, leading to mild iron limitation.
provenance.recipe_evidence: Paper (PMC13185565) Methods: 'grown in MOD medium supplemented with 30 mM glucose (MODG) as the carbon source (ref 32)'; 'Strict anaerobic conditions (pO2 = 0%) were achieved by continuously sparging the medium with pure nitrogen gas.' Ref 32 = Rosenfeld et al. 2005. Composition (compiled from Duport et al. 2004 and the 2025 bioRxiv defined-medium preprint that cites MOD): 'MOD medium consists of a basic medium [45.41 mM (NH4)2SO4, 5.74 mM K2HPO4, 0.16 mM MgSO4] supplemented with trace elements (3.39 mM FeCl2 x 6 H2O, 0.25 mM MnCl2 x 4 H2O, 0.12 mM Na2MoO4 x 2 H2O, 2.47 mM CaCl2, 0.62 mM ZnCl2
provenance.base_medium: MOD medium (chemically defined medium for Bacillus cereus), used as MODG = MOD + 30 mM glucose
provenance.base_medium_reference: Rosenfeld E, Duport C, Zigha A, Schmitt P. 2005. Characterization of aerobic and anaerobic vegetative growth of the food-borne pathogen Bacillus cereus F4430/73 strain. Can J Microbiol 51(2):149-158. doi:10.1139/w04-132 (PMID:15920631). MOD originally derived from Glatz BA, Goepfert JM. 1977 and reformulated by Duport et al. 2004 (Arch Microbiol, doi:10.1007/s00203-004-0688-y). Full salt/trace composition also compiled in the 2025 defined-medium bioRxiv preprint doi:10.1101/2025.07.14.664712.

uncovered.name: amino acid mixture (15 L-amino acids)
